title: Things Fall Apart
author: Chinua Achebe
description: Follows Okonkwo, a proud Igbo leader, as colonial rule and missionary influence unravel his village's traditions.

title: No Longer at Ease
author: Chinua Achebe
description: Traces Okonkwo's grandson Obi as he returns from studying in England and struggles against corruption in colonial Nigeria.

title: Arrow of God
author: Chinua Achebe
description: Depicts a village priest's clash with colonial administrators and his own community over spiritual authority.

title: A Man of the People
author: Chinua Achebe
description: Satirizes post-independence African politics through a corrupt minister and the young teacher who opposes him.

title: Half of a Yellow Sun
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
description: Follows several intertwined lives before and during the Biafran War in the late 1960s.

title: Purple Hibiscus
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
description: A teenage girl navigates a repressive religious household and a country in political turmoil.

title: Americanah
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
description: Explores race, identity, and immigration through a Nigerian woman's life in the US and her return home.

title: The Joys of Motherhood
author: Buchi Emecheta
description: Examines the pressures of motherhood and tradition on a woman navigating colonial-era Lagos.

title: Efuru
author: Flora Nwapa
description: Portrays an independent Igbo woman's life, marriages, and spiritual calling in a changing society.

title: The Famished Road
author: Ben Okri
description: Follows Azaro, a spirit child who chooses to remain in the human world amid poverty and political unrest.

title: Death and the King's Horseman
author: Wole Soyinka
description: Dramatizes the clash between colonial intervention and Yoruba ritual duty surrounding a chief's death.

title: A Dance of the Forests
author: Wole Soyinka
description: Uses ancestral spirits at an independence celebration to question romanticized views of the African past.

title: Season of Migration to the North
author: Tayeb Salih
description: A young Sudanese man returns home and uncovers the dark past of a fellow villager who lived in Europe.

title: So Long a Letter
author: Mariama Ba
description: A widow writes to her friend reflecting on polygamy, grief, and women's roles in post-colonial Senegal.

title: God's Bits of Wood
author: Ousmane Sembene
description: Chronicles a railway workers' strike across French West Africa and its impact on entire communities.

title: Ambiguous Adventure
author: Cheikh Hamidou Kane
description: Follows a young man torn between Islamic upbringing and French colonial education.

title: Petals of Blood
author: Ngugi wa Thiong'o
description: Investigates a murder in a town to expose corruption and betrayal after Kenyan independence.

title: Weep Not, Child
author: Ngugi wa Thiong'o
description: Follows a boy's coming of age during the Mau Mau uprising against British colonial rule.

title: A Grain of Wheat
author: Ngugi wa Thiong'o
description: Set around Kenyan independence day, revealing hidden betrayals from the independence struggle.

title: The River Between
author: Ngugi wa Thiong'o
description: Explores tension between traditional Gikuyu customs and Christian missionary influence in two rival villages.

title: Wizard of the Crow
author: Ngugi wa Thiong'o
description: A sprawling satire of dictatorship in a fictional African state, blending folklore and political critique.

title: Nervous Conditions
author: Tsitsi Dangarembga
description: Follows a young girl's pursuit of education and selfhood under colonial rule and patriarchal expectations.

title: The Book of Not
author: Tsitsi Dangarembga
description: Continues Tambu's story as she faces racism at a mission school during Zimbabwe's liberation war.

title: Disgrace
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: A disgraced professor confronts personal and national reckoning in post-apartheid South Africa.

title: Life & Times of Michael K
author: J.M. Coetzee
description: Follows a gardener's journey through a war-torn, dystopian South Africa in search of peace.

title: Cry, the Beloved Country
author: Alan Paton
description: A Zulu pastor travels to Johannesburg searching for his son amid the injustices of segregation.

title: July's People
author: Nadine Gordimer
description: Imagines a white family fleeing a violent uprising and depending on their former servant for survival.

title: Burger's Daughter
author: Nadine Gordimer
description: Follows the daughter of an anti-apartheid activist as she struggles with inherited political duty.

title: Mine Boy
author: Peter Abrahams
description: Depicts a young man's harsh initiation into mine labor and racial segregation in Johannesburg.

title: Kintu
author: Jennifer Nansubuga Makumbi
description: Traces a curse across generations of a Ugandan family from the 18th century to modern times.

title: The Beautiful Ones Are Not Yet Born
author: Ayi Kwei Armah
description: Follows an unnamed railway clerk resisting the corruption surrounding him in post-independence Ghana.

title: Fragments
author: Ayi Kwei Armah
description: Follows a Ghanaian artist returning from the US and clashing with society's materialistic expectations.

title: Changes: A Love Story
author: Ama Ata Aidoo
description: Follows a professional woman navigating love, independence, and polygamous marriage in modern Ghana.

title: Our Sister Killjoy
author: Ama Ata Aidoo
description: A young Ghanaian woman's journey through Europe exposes racism and cultural dislocation.

title: The Hundred Wells of Salaga
author: Ayesha Harruna Attah
description: Follows two women on opposite sides of the 19th-century West African slave trade.

title: The Palm-Wine Drinkard
author: Amos Tutuola
description: A man journeys through a spirit-filled underworld to bring back his deceased palm-wine tapster.

title: My Life in the Bush of Ghosts
author: Amos Tutuola
description: A young boy lost in a forest of ghosts survives years of surreal encounters before returning home.

title: Sozaboy
author: Ken Saro-Wiwa
description: A naive young man is drawn into civil war violence, narrated in vivid broken English.

title: GraceLand
author: Chris Abani
description: Follows a teenage Elvis impersonator surviving poverty and crime in 1980s Lagos slums.

title: Stay With Me
author: Ayobami Adebayo
description: A couple's marriage is tested by infertility, secrets, and family pressure in 1980s Nigeria.

title: Behold the Dreamers
author: Imbolo Mbue
description: Follows a Cameroonian immigrant family in New York during the 2008 financial crisis.

title: Xala
author: Ousmane Sembene
description: Satirizes a wealthy businessman struck with impotence on his wedding night as a metaphor for a failing nation.

title: Chaka
author: Thomas Mofolo
description: A fictionalized account of the rise and fall of the Zulu king Shaka.

title: Devil on the Cross
author: Ngugi wa Thiong'o
description: A satirical gathering of thieves and exploiters exposes greed in post-colonial Kenya.

title: The Concubine
author: Elechi Amadi
description: A beautiful widow's suitors meet tragic ends in a story rooted in Igbo spirituality and fate.

title: The Interpreters
author: Wole Soyinka
description: Follows a group of young Nigerian intellectuals navigating disillusionment after independence.

title: The Gods Are Not To Blame
author: Ola Rotimi
description: Reimagines the Oedipus myth within a Yoruba kingdom to explore fate and self-deception.

title: King Baabu
author: Wole Soyinka
description: A satirical play mocking a fictional dictator modeled on real African tyrants.

title: The Hairdresser of Harare
author: Tendai Huchu
description: A hairdresser's friendship and rivalry unfold against the backdrop of Zimbabwe's social pressures.

title: We Need New Names
author: NoViolet Bulawayo
description: Follows a girl's childhood in Zimbabwe and later immigrant life in the United States.
